<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient

    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.auth.me_retrieve()

```

<details><summary><code>client.auth.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/auth/client.py">user_permission_update</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Set user permissions
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient, Permission
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.auth.user_permission_update(
    user_id=1,
    user_permissions=[
        Permission(
            codename="codename",
        )
    ],
)

```

```python
)
```

```python
    suite=1,
)
```

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Each restore is wrapped in its own transaction so one conflict
doesn't roll back the others.

</dl>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient

    token="<token>",

)

client.testsuite.suites_bulk_restore_create(

)
```

<details><summary><code>client.testsuite.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/testsuite/client.py">suites_seed_create</a>(...) -> TestSuite</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Trigger seeding of e2e-v2 test suites from the eval service.

Calls the eval service's seed trigger endpoint, which starts a
Temporal workflow to read YAML definitions and create suites + scenarios.

Optional body:
    slugs: list[str] — Filter to specific suite slugs (default: all)
    force_create: bool — Skip idempotency check (default: false)
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.testsuite.suites_seed_create(
    title="title",
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request:** `TestSuite` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">outbound_correlations_create</a>()</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``POST /api/v1/voice/outbound-correlations/``

Records a new correlation row at AWS dispatch time. Idempotent on
both ``aws_contact_id`` (AWS retry double-billing) and
``integration_workflow_id`` (Temporal retry that produced different
AWS ContactIds for the same logical dial).

Request body::

    {
        "aws_contact_id": "<connect-contact-id>",
        "source_phone": "<E.164>",
        "destination_phone": "<E.164>",
        "integration_workflow_id": "<temporal-wf-id>",
        "phone_number_id": <int>,
        "tenant_id": <int>
    }

Responses:
    201 ``{"id": <int>}`` — new row inserted
    200 ``{"id": <int>, "duplicate": "<aws_contact_id|integration_workflow_id>"}`` — idempotent hit
    400 — validation error
    401 — invalid/missing service token
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.outbound_correlations_create()

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">outbound_correlations_abandon_create</a>()</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``POST /api/v1/voice/outbound-correlations/abandon/``

Marks a correlation row as abandoned so future claims for the same
``(source_phone, destination_phone)`` skip it. Called by
integrations-py via voice-py at ``OutboundCallWorkflow`` exit when
no Twilio CallSid was bound to the workflow.

Race precedence: claim wins. The atomic UPDATE uses
``WHERE claimed_at IS NULL AND abandoned_at IS NULL`` so an
in-flight claim that committed between the workflow's exit
decision and this request lands first; the abandon then no-ops
and we return ``{"abandoned": false, "reason": "already_claimed"}``.

Best-effort: failures here don't block the workflow. The retention
sweeper (3-day TTL) is the durable safety net — abandon is purely
opportunistic cleanup that releases the (source, destination) slot
sooner than ``claim_expires_at`` (2 min) would.

Tenant scoping: integration_workflow_id is globally unique, so we
don't require a tenant_id parameter — the row's tenant is implicit
in the workflow id. (Same pattern as record_outbound_correlation's
tenant inference from phone_number_id.)

Request body::

    {
        "integration_workflow_id": "<temporal-wf-id>",
        "reason": "<short_tag>",  # optional, defaults to ""
    }

Responses:
    200 ``{"abandoned": true,  "id": <int>}`` — row marked
    200 ``{"abandoned": false, "reason": "already_claimed"}``
    200 ``{"abandoned": false, "reason": "not_found"}``
    200 ``{"abandoned": false, "reason": "already_abandoned"}``
    400 — validation error
    401 — invalid/missing service token
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.outbound_correlations_abandon_create()

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">outbound_correlations_by_call_sid_retrieve</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``GET /api/v1/voice/outbound-correlations/by-call-sid/<call_sid>/?tenant_id=<int>``

Look up an already-claimed correlation row by its Twilio CallSid so
the voice-py status-callback handler can forward
``on_call_status`` to the integrations-py ``OutboundCallWorkflow``.
Without this signal the workflow blocks on ``wait_condition`` until
its ``run_timeout_seconds`` elapses and resolves as ``timeout``,
even when ``on_call_enrichment`` has already arrived.

Service-token auth + **mandatory tenant scoping** via the
``tenant_id`` query param. CallSids are not secret (they appear in
Twilio console, status callbacks, and logs), so a global lookup
would let any service-token holder probe known CallSids and
enumerate correlations across tenants. The caller (voice-py) has
``tenant_id`` from the cached session row, so the requirement is
free at the call site.

Responses:
    200 ``{"integration_workflow_id": <str>, "tenant_id": <int>}``
    404 — no row claimed under that call_sid for the given tenant
    400 — missing/non-integer tenant_id query param
    401 — invalid/missing service token
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.outbound_correlations_by_call_sid_retrieve(
    call_sid="call_sid",
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**call_sid:** `str` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">outbound_correlations_claim_create</a>()</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``POST /api/v1/voice/outbound-correlations/claim/``

Atomically claims the oldest unclaimed correlation matching
``(source_phone, destination_phone)`` whose ``claim_expires_at`` is
still in the future (Phase 14: column-driven 2-min TTL replaces
the legacy 180s ``created_at``-derived window).

Reconnect path: if a row with this ``twilio_call_sid`` already
exists (Twilio webhook retry on the same call), return its
``integration_workflow_id`` directly without re-claiming. The
reconnect lookup is intentionally NOT gated on
``claim_expires_at`` / ``abandoned_at`` — once a row is claimed
its workflow id is the durable answer for that CallSid until
retention expires, regardless of TTL/abandon state.

Single-statement claim: ``UPDATE ... WHERE id=(SELECT ... FOR
UPDATE SKIP LOCKED LIMIT 1) RETURNING ...``. Survives Supabase
transaction-mode pooling because it's one round-trip; no FOR UPDATE
held across statements.

Request body::

    {
        "source_phone": "<Twilio.To, E.164>",
        "destination_phone": "<Twilio.From, E.164>",
        "twilio_call_sid": "<CA...>",
        "tenant_id": <int>
    }

Responses:
    200 ``{"integration_workflow_id": "<id>"}`` — hit
    204 — no eligible row in the claim window
    401 — invalid/missing service token
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.outbound_correlations_claim_create()

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">outbound_correlations_sweep_create</a>()</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``POST /api/v1/voice/outbound-correlations/sweep/``

Phase 14: deletes correlation rows past their
``retention_expires_at`` deadline. Service-token only — designed
to be invoked by a Temporal scheduled workflow (see
``ScheduledTriggerWorkflow``) on a daily cadence at low traffic.

Cross-tenant by design (the sweeper has no tenant context and
retention is uniform across tenants); ``disable_auto_filters``
bypasses the tenant ContextVar manager filter so all expired
rows surface for the same DELETE.

The implementation mirrors ``reap_outbound_correlations`` so an
operator can either run the management command on a host (e.g.
one-shot ad-hoc cleanup) OR rely on the Temporal-driven HTTP
path for production. Both paths converge on the same
``retention_expires_at < now()`` predicate.

Hard upper bound: ``MAX_DELETES_PER_REQUEST`` caps the total number
of rows a single sweep call may delete (defense-in-depth against a
misconfigured schedule firing every minute or a token-compromise
blast). Operators can re-invoke the endpoint or run the
``reap_outbound_correlations`` management command for unbounded
one-shot cleanups.

Request body: ``{"batch_size": <int, default=1000>}`` (optional)
Response: 200 ``{"deleted": <int>, "cutoff": "<ISO>", "capped": <bool>}``
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.outbound_correlations_sweep_create()

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">phone_numbers_config_retrieve</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Return non-credential phone number config (iPaaS service-token only).

Used by the iPaaS pre-call pipeline to derive ``from_phone`` and the
default outbound workflow id from a ``phone_number_id``, so schedules
only need ``phone_number_id`` instead of duplicating these fields.

Distinct from ``PhoneNumberAuthView`` (which exposes Twilio credentials
to voice-py via ``ZENAPI_VOICE_SERVICE_TOKEN``) — this endpoint:
- NEVER returns auth_token/account_sid
- Authenticates via ``ZENAPI_INTEGRATIONS_SERVICE_TOKEN`` (iPaaS identity)

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">phone_numbers_by_phone_retrieve</a>()</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``GET /api/v1/voice/phone-numbers/by-phone/?phone=<E.164>``

Lightweight service-token lookup that resolves an E.164 phone string
to the tenant + ``phone_number_id`` of an ``AwsConnectPhoneNumber``
row that owns it. Matches on either ``bridge_phone`` (the Twilio
BYOC bridge number AWS Connect dials) OR ``phone_number`` (the AWS
caller-ID for single-number deploys).

Used by voice-py's Twilio BYOC inbound webhook (ZEN-205) to get
tenant context BEFORE creating a session, so it can claim the
outbound correlation and ``attach_call_sid`` to the pre-existing
dispatch session — the bridge that makes per-call ``variables``
reach the bot for AWS-Connect-originated calls.

Non-AWS phones return 404. Voice-py treats 404 as "this isn't a
BYOC bridge inbound, fall through to legacy session-creation
path." That preserves regular Twilio inbound behavior.

Phone numbers are globally unique (``BasePhoneNumber`` has a
unique constraint on ``phone_number`` for ``is_deleted=False``),
so at most one row matches per query — no cross-tenant
disambiguation needed.

Responses:
    200 ``{"tenant_id": <int>, "phone_number_id": <int>, "provider": "amazon_connect"}``
    404 — no AwsConnectPhoneNumber owns this number
    400 — missing/empty ``phone`` query param
    401 — invalid/missing service token
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.phone_numbers_by_phone_retrieve()

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

Call outcome

Computed call disposition category (ZEN-71). The matching disposition_sentiment is derived server-side — clients should not send it.

* `hangup_immediate` - Hangup Immediate

</dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**summary_pending:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Reliability marker for the post-call summary pipeline (architect H7). Voice-py sets True alongside the terminal status update — BEFORE the inline summary path starts — so an OOM-kill between the status update and the summary path still leaves a breadcrumb the reconciler can sweep. Cleared on successful summary persist.

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">sessions_terminal_by_workflow_create</a>()</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

``POST /api/v1/voice/sessions/terminal-by-workflow/``

Marks a ``VoiceInteractionSession`` terminal when no bot lifecycle
will write it (AWS Connect no-answer/busy/failed paths). Looks up
the session via the ZEN-204 ``OutboundCallCorrelation.session_id``
bridge, atomically updates the parent + child tables under a
WHERE-IN guard so already-active rows are no-ops.

Tenant scoping: ``integration_workflow_id`` is globally unique, so
the row's tenant is implicit (same pattern as the abandon endpoint).
Service-token auth bypasses the RLS middleware; we set
``app.current_tenant_id`` GUC explicitly inside the transaction.

Request body::

    {
        "integration_workflow_id": "<temporal-wf-id>",
        "call_outcome": "no_answer" | "busy" | "failed" | "canceled" | "answered" | ...,
        "duration_seconds": <int>,           # optional, default 0
        "raw_disconnect_reason": "<str>"     # optional, default ""
    }

Responses (always 200 — best-effort writer; failures are diagnostic):
    ``{"updated": true,  "session_id": <int>, "reason": "updated"}``
    ``{"updated": false, "reason": "correlation_not_found"}``
    ``{"updated": false, "reason": "no_session_bound"}``  # pre-ZEN-204 voice-py
    ``{"updated": false, "session_id": <int>, "reason": "already_terminal"}``
    400 — validation error
    401 — invalid/missing service token
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.sessions_terminal_by_workflow_create()

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.voice.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/voice/client.py">transcribers_switch_resourcetype_create</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Replace a transcriber row with a different child type.

The ``workflow`` OneToOneField unique constraint blocks an
insert-then-delete approach, so we hard-delete the old row inside
an atomic block and recreate via the polymorphic serializer.
``resourcetype`` in the payload picks the new child type; the rest
of the body is forwarded to the serializer for validation.

Accepted ``resourcetype`` values come from
``TranscriberPolymorphicSerializer.valid_resourcetypes()``, derived
from ``model_serializer_mapping`` — adding a new child type only
requires a mapping entry.

Field-carryover semantics: any field omitted from the payload would
otherwise reset to the model default on the freshly inserted row.
For workspace-secret links (``api_key_secret``) and the human
``title`` we explicitly carry the value over from the old row when
the payload omits the key — so callers (UI or API) don't silently
drop the secret link or the user-given name on a type switch.
Explicit ``"api_key_secret": null`` / ``"title": null`` in the body
still clear them (we use key-presence, not truthiness, so explicit-
null is honored).

Errors during serializer validation propagate as
``ValidationError`` (400) and Django auto-rolls back the txn.

.. note::
    ``QuerySet.delete()`` fires ``pre_delete``/``post_delete`` signals on
    the old row. Receivers that perform external side effects (webhooks,
    cleanup tasks) will trigger spuriously on every type switch. If you
    add such a receiver, gate it on ``not getattr(instance, "_swapping",
    False)`` or similar.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.voice.transcribers_switch_resourcetype_create(
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Base Transcriber.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_export_retrieve</a>(...) -> str</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Export a workflow as a portable payload. No PKs, tenant refs, or API keys are included. Default format is YAML; pass ?format=json for JSON. The response carries a Content-Disposition: attachment header so browsers prompt a download.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_export_retrieve(
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**format:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Output format: 'yaml' (default) or 'json'.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

```python
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_nodes_tools_update(
    id=1,
    node_pk=1,

    ],
)
```

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_business_hours_list</a>(...) -> PaginatedBusinessHoursConfigList</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for BusinessHoursConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

Since a workflow has at most one business hours config, this behaves as
a singleton resource under the workflow:

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — delete
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_business_hours_list(
    workflow_pk=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

</dd>
</dl>

<dl>

</dd>
</dl>

#### 📝 Description

CRUD for BusinessHoursConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

Since a workflow has at most one business hours config, this behaves as
a singleton resource under the workflow:

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — delete

```python
client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_business_hours_retrieve(
    workflow_pk=1,
    id=1,
)

```

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_business_hours_destroy</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for BusinessHoursConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

Since a workflow has at most one business hours config, this behaves as
a singleton resource under the workflow:

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — delete
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_business_hours_destroy(
    workflow_pk=1,
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Business Hours Config.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for BusinessHoursConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

Since a workflow has at most one business hours config, this behaves as
a singleton resource under the workflow:

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/      — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/business-hours/{pk}/  — delete
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

    token="<token>",

)

client.workflow.workflows_business_hours_partial_update(
```

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_call_summary_config_list</a>(...) -> PaginatedWorkflowCallSummaryConfigList</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for WorkflowCallSummaryConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

A workflow has at most one call-summary config, so this behaves as a
singleton resource under the workflow (mirrors
``FatalErrorEscalationConfigViewSet``):

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/          — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/          — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — delete
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/resolved/ — effective config

The ``resolved`` action returns the cascaded effective config (workflow →
tenant → system default) that integrations-py reads to decide whether and
how to generate a post-call summary.

Tenant isolation: ``WorkflowCallSummaryConfig`` inherits
``ActivityTenantBaseModel`` whose default manager auto-scopes queries to
the current tenant; the ``_get_workflow`` helper ANDs an explicit
``tenant=`` predicate on top for defense in depth. Verified by
``TestCallSummaryConfigTenantIsolation``.

Auth: inherits ``viewsets.ModelViewSet`` (not the project's
``BaseModelViewSet``) intentionally — matches sibling
``FatalErrorEscalationConfigViewSet`` and relies on the global
``DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES`` (Knox token) +
``DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES`` (IsAuthenticated + CustomModelPermissions)
in ``django_drf.py``. Switching to ``BaseModelViewSet`` is a no-op for
auth here; keeping ``ModelViewSet`` avoids dragging in the base class's
list-action filter assumptions on a singleton resource.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_call_summary_config_list(
    workflow_pk=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**page:** `typing.Optional[int]` — A page number within the paginated result set.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**page_size:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Number of results to return per page.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_call_summary_config_partial_update</a>(...) -> WorkflowCallSummaryConfig</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for WorkflowCallSummaryConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

A workflow has at most one call-summary config, so this behaves as a
singleton resource under the workflow (mirrors
``FatalErrorEscalationConfigViewSet``):

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/          — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/          — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/{pk}/     — delete
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/call-summary-config/resolved/ — effective config

The ``resolved`` action returns the cascaded effective config (workflow →
tenant → system default) that integrations-py reads to decide whether and
how to generate a post-call summary.

Tenant isolation: ``WorkflowCallSummaryConfig`` inherits
``ActivityTenantBaseModel`` whose default manager auto-scopes queries to
the current tenant; the ``_get_workflow`` helper ANDs an explicit
``tenant=`` predicate on top for defense in depth. Verified by
``TestCallSummaryConfigTenantIsolation``.

Auth: inherits ``viewsets.ModelViewSet`` (not the project's
``BaseModelViewSet``) intentionally — matches sibling
``FatalErrorEscalationConfigViewSet`` and relies on the global
``DEFAULT_AUTHENTICATION_CLASSES`` (Knox token) +
``DEFAULT_PERMISSION_CLASSES`` (IsAuthenticated + CustomModelPermissions)
in ``django_drf.py``. Switching to ``BaseModelViewSet`` is a no-op for
auth here; keeping ``ModelViewSet`` avoids dragging in the base class's
list-action filter assumptions on a singleton resource.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)
```

```python
client.workflow.workflows_call_summary_config_partial_update(
    workflow_pk=1,
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Call Summary Config.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Whether to generate a post-call summary. Unset inherits from the next tier (workflow → tenant → system default, which is ON).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**prompt_override:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Instruction text override for the summarizer. Unset inherits the next tier's prompt, falling back to the system default prompt.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**min_transcript_turns:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Minimum number of meaningful customer turns required before a summary is generated. Unset inherits from the next tier (system default is 1).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**model_override:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Specific LLM model to use for summarization. Unset inherits from the next tier (system default is None → use the workflow's LLM). Note: this selects a model WITHIN the env-resolved provider's namespace — the provider (OpenAI / Gemini / OpenRouter) is chosen by env (CALL_SUMMARY_PROVIDER / key presence), not per-config.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**max_summary_chars:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Truncation guard for the one-sentence summary. Unset inherits from the next tier (system default is None → no truncation).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**max_description_chars:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Truncation guard for the detailed description. Unset inherits from the next tier (system default is None → no truncation).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_edges_conditions_list</a>(...) -> PaginatedEdgeConditionGroupList</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for EdgeConditionGroups nested under an edge.

Standard REST endpoints:
    GET    /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/        — list groups
    POST   /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/        — create single group
    GET    /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — retrieve group
    PUT    /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — update group
    PATCH  /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — partial update group
    DELETE /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — delete group

Bulk endpoint:
    PUT /edges/{edge_pk}/conditions/bulk/
    Accepts an array of condition group objects with nested conditions.
    Performs a full replacement: creates new, updates existing (by id),
    deletes any not in the payload.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python

    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_edges_conditions_list(
    workflow_pk="workflow_pk",
    edge_pk=1,
)

```

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_edges_validate_else_create</a>(...) -> Edge</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Validate that all rule-based nodes in the workflow have an else edge.

Called by the UI before publishing/activating a workflow to ensure
no rule-based node is missing a fallback edge.

Returns 200 with {valid: true} or 400 with details of which nodes
are missing an else edge.
</dd>

</dd>
</dl>

```python
)

client.workflow.workflows_edges_validate_else_create(
    workflow_pk=1,
    from_node=1,
    to_node=1,
)

```

    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/escalation-config/     — create

    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/escalation-config/{pk}/ — full update

</dd>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)
```

```python
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Escalation Config.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Enable global agent/human request handling across all nodes.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**counter:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Number of pushback attempts before escalating. 0 = transfer immediately on first request.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**pushback_mode:** `typing.Optional[PushbackModeEnum]` 

How pushback responses are generated: 'flexible' = LLM contextualizes a prompt, 'exact' = ordered list of exact scripts.

* `flexible` - LLM Contextualized (Recommended)
* `exact` - Exact Script List
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**pushback_prompt:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Prompt the LLM uses to generate contextual pushback responses (used when pushback_mode is 'flexible').
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**pushback_scripts:** `typing.Optional[typing.Any]` — Ordered list of exact pushback scripts, one per attempt (used when pushback_mode is 'exact'). Length should equal counter.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**transfer_number:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Reference to a centrally-managed transfer number. Takes precedence over the legacy phone_number/phone_extension fields.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**phone_number:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Legacy: phone number for escalation. Use transfer_number FK instead.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**phone_extension:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Legacy: extension for escalation. Use transfer_number FK instead.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**transfer_message:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Message spoken to the caller before transferring. If empty, a default message is used.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**transcription_enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Whether to enable transcription during the transfer call leg.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_fatal_error_escalation_config_list</a>(...) -> PaginatedFatalErrorEscalationConfigList</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for FatalErrorEscalationConfig nested under a workflow (OneToOne).

Since a workflow has at most one fatal-error escalation config (ZEN-56),
this behaves as a singleton resource under the workflow:

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/      — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/      — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — delete

Inheritance note: extends ``viewsets.ModelViewSet`` directly (not the
project's ``BaseModelViewSet``). This matches the sibling
``EscalationConfigViewSet`` convention — both nested bot-level config
viewsets use ``pk`` URL kwargs rather than ``BaseModelViewSet``'s
``lookup_url_kwarg = "id"``. Keeping both consistent avoids per-viewset
URL-kwarg divergence; migrating both to ``BaseModelViewSet`` is a
separate repo-wide cleanup.

Tenant isolation: ``FatalErrorEscalationConfig`` inherits
``ActivityTenantBaseModel``, whose default manager is an
``AutoFilteringManager`` wrapping ``TenantAutoFilter``
(see ``zenlib/reusable_apps/multitenant/models.py``). All queries
made via ``FatalErrorEscalationConfig.objects`` are auto-scoped to the
current tenant set by ``MultitenantContextMiddleware`` — the explicit
``.filter(workflow_id=...)`` in ``get_queryset`` ANDs on top of that.
Cross-tenant isolation is verified by
``TestFatalErrorEscalationConfigTenantIsolation``.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_fatal_error_escalation_config_list(
    workflow_pk=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

<dd>

Since a workflow has at most one fatal-error escalation config (ZEN-56),
this behaves as a singleton resource under the workflow:

    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/      — list (0 or 1)
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/      — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/fatal-error-escalation-config/{pk}/ — delete

Inheritance note: extends ``viewsets.ModelViewSet`` directly (not the
project's ``BaseModelViewSet``). This matches the sibling
``EscalationConfigViewSet`` convention — both nested bot-level config
viewsets use ``pk`` URL kwargs rather than ``BaseModelViewSet``'s
``lookup_url_kwarg = "id"``. Keeping both consistent avoids per-viewset
URL-kwarg divergence; migrating both to ``BaseModelViewSet`` is a
separate repo-wide cleanup.

Tenant isolation: ``FatalErrorEscalationConfig`` inherits
``ActivityTenantBaseModel``, whose default manager is an
``AutoFilteringManager`` wrapping ``TenantAutoFilter``
(see ``zenlib/reusable_apps/multitenant/models.py``). All queries
made via ``FatalErrorEscalationConfig.objects`` are auto-scoped to the
current tenant set by ``MultitenantContextMiddleware`` — the explicit
``.filter(workflow_id=...)`` in ``get_queryset`` ANDs on top of that.
Cross-tenant isolation is verified by
``TestFatalErrorEscalationConfigTenantIsolation``.

URL-kwarg divergence; migrating both to ``BaseModelViewSet`` is a
separate repo-wide cleanup.

Tenant isolation: ``FatalErrorEscalationConfig`` inherits
``ActivityTenantBaseModel``, whose default manager is an
``AutoFilteringManager`` wrapping ``TenantAutoFilter``
(see ``zenlib/reusable_apps/multitenant/models.py``). All queries
made via ``FatalErrorEscalationConfig.objects`` are auto-scoped to the
current tenant set by ``MultitenantContextMiddleware`` — the explicit

</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_fatal_error_escalation_config_partial_update(
    workflow_pk=1,
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Fatal Error Escalation Config.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Enable fallback redirect on fatal STT/LLM/TTS errors.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**fallback_transfer_number:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Reference to a centrally-managed transfer number used as the fallback destination when a fatal system error is detected. PROTECT prevents removing a TransferNumber that is wired as a safety net — admin must disable or reassign the config first.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**speak_error_message:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If True, the voice agent speaks the error_message (or a default apology) before redirecting. If False, the redirect is silent — no TTS is played at all. Useful when the TTS provider is the failing service or when customers prefer a clean handoff.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**error_message:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Message spoken to the caller before the fallback transfer. If empty, a default apology is used. Ignored when speak_error_message is False.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**respect_business_hours:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If True, out-of-hours fatal errors play the OOH message and hang up (matches ZEN-54 semantics). If False, the fallback transfer fires regardless of business hours.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for GlobalListenerPhrase nested under a global listener.

GET    /.../{listener_pk}/phrases/      — list
POST   /.../{listener_pk}/phrases/      — create
GET    /.../{listener_pk}/phrases/{pk}/  — retrieve
PUT    /.../{listener_pk}/phrases/{pk}/  — full update
PATCH  /.../{listener_pk}/phrases/{pk}/  — partial update
DELETE /.../{listener_pk}/phrases/{pk}/  — delete
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_global_listener_phrases_partial_update(
    workflow_pk="workflow_pk",
    globallistener_pk="globallistener_pk",
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `str` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**globallistener_pk:** `str` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Global Listener Phrase.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**phrase:** `typing.Optional[str]` — The trigger phrase to listen for (e.g. 'do not call me again').
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**description:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Admin note describing what this phrase trigger does.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**action_type:** `typing.Optional[ActionTypeEnum]` 

What happens when the phrase is detected.

* `goto_node` - Go to Node
* `transfer` - Transfer Call
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**target_node:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Node to route to when phrase is detected (goto_node action).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**transfer_number:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Transfer number to call when phrase is detected (transfer action).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**transfer_message:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Message spoken to the caller before transferring.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**acknowledgement_message:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Message spoken to the caller before taking any action. If empty, the action is taken immediately.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**fire_once:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If True, the phrase is disabled after the first trigger within the same call session.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**requires_confirmation:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If True, the bot asks for confirmation before executing the action. Stays on the current node during confirmation.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**confirmation_prompt:** `typing.Optional[str]` — The question asked to confirm the user's intent. Required when requires_confirmation is True. Example: 'Are you sure you want to be placed on our do-not-call list?'
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**confirmation_max_retries:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Number of re-ask attempts on unclear responses before auto-denying and resuming the conversation. Default 2 means 3 total asks (1 initial + 2 retries).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**order:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Display order in the UI.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**scope:** `typing.Optional[GlobalListenerPhraseScopeEnum]` 

Whether this phrase is monitored on every node (all_nodes) or only on the nodes in scope_nodes (specific_nodes).

* `all_nodes` - All Nodes
* `specific_nodes` - Specific Nodes
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**scope_node_ids:** `typing.Optional[typing.List[int]]` — Node ids this phrase is scoped to (scope='specific_nodes').
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

**step_type:** `typing.Optional[WorkflowWorkflowsNodesInstructionStepsListRequestStepType]` 

Discriminator — set automatically by each subclass.

* `speak` - Speak
* `collect` - Collect Variable
* `set_value` - Set Value
* `bulk_set_value` - Bulk Set Value
* `condition` - Condition (If/Else)
* `transfer` - Transfer
* `run_tool` - Run Tool (Deterministic)
* `fuzzy_match` - Fuzzy Match
* `custom_prompt` - Custom Prompt
* `end_conversation` - End Conversation

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_nodes_instruction_steps_conditions_list</a>(...) -> PaginatedStepConditionGroupList</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for StepConditionGroups nested under a ConditionStep.

Standard REST endpoints:
    GET    /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/        — list groups
    POST   /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/        — create group
    GET    /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — retrieve group
    PUT    /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — update group
    PATCH  /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — partial group
    DELETE /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/{pk}/   — delete group

Bulk endpoint:
    PUT /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/conditions/bulk/
    Full replacement: creates new groups, updates by id, deletes absent.

</dd>

<dd>

**title:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Human-readable label for this condition branch (e.g. 'Premium Customer', 'High Priority').
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**order:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Evaluation order among groups (lower = earlier). Groups are OR'd together.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**is_else:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If True, this is the default/else branch (conditions are ignored, branch_steps are used as fallback).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**branch_end_behavior:** `typing.Optional[StepConditionGroupBranchEndBehaviorEnum]` 

What happens when branch steps complete without a terminal action: return to main instructions or go to a step.

* `return_to_main` - Return to Main Instructions
* `goto_step` - Go to Specific Step
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Run Tool Step Input.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**scenario_input_key:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Matches a key in the Tool's wrapped scenario.input_schema (and scenario.trigger_input).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**binding_source:** `typing.Optional[BindingSourceEnum]` 

Only 'variable_bound' and 'constant' are accepted at the step site — 'llm_supplied' is rejected at save time.

* `llm_supplied` - LLM-Supplied
* `variable_bound` - Bound to Session Variable
* `constant` - Constant
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**variable:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Required when binding_source=variable_bound; forbidden otherwise.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**constant_value:** `typing.Optional[typing.Any]` — Required when binding_source=constant; forbidden otherwise.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>

    PATCH  /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/writebacks/{pk}/   — partial update
    DELETE /instruction-steps/{step_pk}/writebacks/{pk}/   — delete

<dd>

CRUD for VariableOptions nested under a variable.

Standard REST endpoints:
    GET    /variables/{variable_pk}/options/        — list
    POST   /variables/{variable_pk}/options/        — create single
    GET    /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — retrieve
    PUT    /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — update
    PATCH  /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — partial update
    DELETE /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — delete

Bulk endpoint:
    PUT /variables/{variable_pk}/options/bulk/
    Accepts an array of options. Performs a full replacement:
    creates new, updates existing (by id), deletes any not in the payload.

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_nodes_variables_options_destroy</a>(...)</code></summary>
<dl>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for VariableOptions nested under a variable.

Standard REST endpoints:
    GET    /variables/{variable_pk}/options/        — list
    POST   /variables/{variable_pk}/options/        — create single
    GET    /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — retrieve
    PUT    /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — update
    PATCH  /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — partial update
    DELETE /variables/{variable_pk}/options/{pk}/   — delete

Bulk endpoint:
    PUT /variables/{variable_pk}/options/bulk/

    creates new, updates existing (by id), deletes any not in the payload.

```python
)

client.workflow.workflows_nodes_variables_options_bulk_update(
    workflow_pk="workflow_pk",
    node_pk="node_pk",

)
```

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

CRUD for TransferNumber nested under a workflow.

Endpoints:
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/transfer-numbers/          — list
    POST   /workflows/{workflow_pk}/transfer-numbers/          — create
    GET    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/transfer-numbers/{pk}/     — retrieve
    PUT    /workflows/{workflow_pk}/transfer-numbers/{pk}/     — full update
    PATCH  /workflows/{workflow_pk}/transfer-numbers/{pk}/     — partial update
    DELETE /workflows/{workflow_pk}/transfer-numbers/{pk}/     — delete
</dd>
</dl>

</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_transfer_numbers_partial_update(
    workflow_pk=1,
    id=1,
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**workflow_pk:** `int` 
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**id:** `int` — A unique integer value identifying this Transfer Number.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**name:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Human-readable label (e.g. 'Sales Desk', 'Support Line').
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**phone_number:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +14155551234).
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**extension:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Optional DTMF extension to dial after transfer.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**is_fallback:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — If True, this number is the fallback escalation destination. At most one transfer number per workflow can be the fallback.

</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**transcription_enabled:** `typing.Optional[bool]` — Whether to enable transcription on the transferred call leg.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**order:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Display ordering in the Transfer Setup UI.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>

#### 📝 Description

<dd>

<dd>

</dl>

<dd>

<dd>

</dl>

<dd>

</dd>
</dl>
</dd>

<details><summary><code>client.workflow.<a href="src/zenlabs_sdk/workflow/client.py">workflows_import_create</a>(...) -> Workflow</code></summary>
<dl>
<dd>

#### 📝 Description

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

Import a workflow from a YAML file. Response shape: {workflow, issues, post_import_actions}. Use on_conflict=replace to mutate an existing workflow in place (PK preserved). Use on_conflict=abort to 409 on collision.
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### 🔌 Usage

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

```python
from zenlabs_sdk import ZenClient
from zenlabs_sdk.environment import ZenClientEnvironment

client = ZenClient(
    token="<token>",
    environment=ZenClientEnvironment.PRODUCTION,
)

client.workflow.workflows_import_create(
    file="file",
)

```
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>

#### ⚙️ Parameters

<dl>
<dd>

<dl>
<dd>

**file:** `str` — A YAML file previously exported via the export endpoint.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**on_conflict:** `typing.Optional[OnConflictEnum]` 

How to handle a workflow with the same title in the target tenant. 'create_new' (default) appends ' (Imported)'. 'replace' mutates the existing workflow in place (PK preserved). 'abort' returns 409 on collision.

* `create_new` - create_new
* `replace` - replace
* `abort` - abort
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**name:** `typing.Optional[str]` — Optional title override. Used as the final workflow title (no suffix added) and also as the value used for the collision check.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**target_tenant_id:** `typing.Optional[int]` — Staff-only. Target tenant id for the import. If omitted, the caller's tenant context is used.
    
</dd>
</dl>

<dl>
<dd>

**request_options:** `typing.Optional[RequestOptions]` — Request-specific configuration.
    
</dd>
</dl>
</dd>
</dl>


</dd>
</dl>
</details>
